QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_33

question.text: AUM (Assets Under Management) of a mutual fund scheme represents:

answer.text: The total amount originally invested by all unitholders
The face value of all units multiplied by the number of units
The historical cost of all securities held in the portfolio
The market value of the scheme's portfolio, marked to market daily

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AUM represents the current market value of all investments held by the scheme, marked to market daily. It changes with market movements and net fund flows (purchases minus redemptions). **The Trap:** AUM is NOT the total money invested (which would be the cost basis) or the face value of units. It is the current market value, which fluctuates daily. **Cross-Exam Context:** AUM is the base for TER calculations and is reported by AMFI monthly. NISM Series V-A and II-B both test the understanding of AUM computation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When comparing funds, look at AUM trends. Consistently growing AUM may indicate investor confidence, but extremely large AUM in small/mid cap funds can become a performance drag due to reduced investment flexibility.
